hero.title: 5 Days Jogja
hero.titleAccent: Borobudur, Prambanan & The Land Above the Clouds
hero.intro: The most comprehensive Yogyakarta journey available — spanning volcanic jeep tours on Merapi, the world's greatest Buddhist temple at Borobudur, Indonesia's most beautiful Hindu complex at Prambanan, the thrill of Timang Beach, the magic of the Dieng Plateau's ancient temples in the clouds, and the royal heart of Javanese civilisation. Five extraordinary days from just RM1,850 per person.

breadcrumb.label: 5 Days Jogja — Land Above the Clouds

hero.heroImageAlt: Dieng Plateau ancient temples above the clouds, Central Java

includes: All meals as specified (breakfast, lunch, dinner daily)

overview.heading: The Ultimate 5-Day Yogyakarta Experience — From Volcanic Craters to Ancient Temples in the Clouds
body: Yogyakarta is one of Southeast Asia's richest travel destinations — a city where 9th-century Buddhist and Hindu monuments stand within an hour of each other, where an active stratovolcano looms over rice paddies, where royal palace traditions carry on unchanged for centuries, and where a mystical highland plateau sits above the clouds with temples older than Borobudur itself. This 5-day package is designed for travellers who want to experience the full spectrum of what Yogyakarta and Central Java have to offer.

The itinerary builds from the spectacular to the sublime: Day 1 begins with Borobudur and the famous Malioboro Street; Day 2 delivers the volcanic thrill of Mount Merapi followed by Prambanan and Taman Sari; Day 3 ventures to Timang Beach's dramatic cliffs and the serene Pengger Pine Forest; Day 4 makes the full-day pilgrimage to the extraordinary Dieng Plateau — Java's 'Land Above the Clouds' — with its ancient Hindu temples, colour-changing crater lake, and sulphuric volcanic vents; and Day 5 provides a relaxed farewell before departure. Every meal is included, all tours are 100% private, and the price includes every entrance fee on the itinerary.

highlights.heading: Five Days of Extraordinary Java Experiences

items.title: Borobudur — World's Largest Buddhist Monument
items.body: No superlative adequately captures Borobudur. Built in the 9th century by the Sailendra dynasty and declared a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1991, this nine-tiered pyramid covers 2,500 square metres and is decorated with 2,672 narrative bas-relief panels and 504 seated Buddhas. From the circular upper terraces, the view encompasses five volcanoes and the entire Kedu Plain — a panorama that has inspired pilgrims and travellers for over a thousand years.
items.imageAlt: Borobudur temple terraces at sunrise, Kedu Plain

items.title: Mount Merapi 4WD Jeep Lava Tour
items.body: Java's most active volcano has erupted more than 68 times since the 16th century, most recently in a catastrophic 2010 event that killed 350 people. Today its hardened lava fields form a dramatic landscape of alien rock formations, buried bunkers and eerie museum exhibits. An open 4WD jeep takes you through these geological badlands with a guide who lived through the eruption — making this one of the most viscerally powerful experiences in Indonesian travel.
items.imageAlt: Mount Merapi 4WD jeep lava tour, Yogyakarta

items.title: Prambanan — Indonesia's Most Beautiful Hindu Temple
items.body: The Prambanan compound's three central towers soar to 47 metres above the Javanese plain, their volcanic andesite facades covered in exquisite Ramayana relief carvings. Built around 850 CE by the Hindu Mataram kingdom as a rival to the Buddhist Borobudur, Prambanan is designated a UNESCO World Heritage Site and hosts the spectacular open-air Ramayana Ballet on selected evenings. Combined with a Taman Sari Water Castle visit on the same afternoon, Day 2 in this package is among the most culturally rich days possible in Java.
items.imageAlt: Prambanan Hindu temple towers at dusk, Yogyakarta

items.title: Dieng Plateau — The Land Above the Clouds
items.body: At 2,000 metres above sea level in the Wonosobo regency of Central Java, the Dieng Plateau is one of Indonesia's most otherworldly destinations. Ancient Hindu temples (predating Borobudur) rise from misty highland meadows; the Sikidang Crater exhales sulphurous steam from the active caldera floor; and the famous Telaga Warna (Colour Lake) shifts between turquoise, green and golden hues depending on the mineral content and time of day. Dieng's sacred springs and mystical atmosphere have drawn Javanese pilgrims for over 1,200 years.
items.imageAlt: Dieng Plateau ancient temples and Telaga Warna colour lake, Central Java

items.title: Timang Beach & Pengger Pine Forest
items.body: Day 3 ventures to the dramatic coast of Gunungkidul: Timang Beach's sheer limestone cliffs above the crashing Indian Ocean provide an optional gondola ride to Watu Panjang Island (own expense). The afternoon winds through the magical Pengger Pine Forest on the Dlingo ridge — tall pines, Instagram-famous viewing platforms and a golden-hour light that silhouettes the trees perfectly against the volcanic landscape below.
items.imageAlt: Timang Beach dramatic limestone cliffs, Gunungkidul, Yogyakarta

items.title: Hobbit House & Malioboro Street
items.body: Day 3's adventure also includes a visit to the famous 'Hobbit House' — a Lord of the Rings-inspired grass-roofed hobbit hole tucked into the Gunungkidul hills, beloved by families and creative travellers. The first and last days both feature Malioboro Street, Yogyakarta's legendary 1-kilometre batik-and-craft market strip, where everything from handmade wayang puppets to artisan silver can be found at remarkably good prices.
items.imageAlt: Malioboro Street Yogyakarta batik market at dusk

days.title: Arrive Yogyakarta — Borobudur Temple — Beringharjo Market — Malioboro Street
days.description: Your English-speaking private driver-guide meets you at Yogyakarta International Airport (YIA) and after a welcome Javanese lunch, head northwest for Borobudur — the world's largest Buddhist monument and a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Spend 1.5 to 2 hours exploring its nine ascending terraces, running your hands along 2,672 carved relief panels and pausing among the 72 perforated bell stupas on the circular upper platforms for sweeping views across the volcanic Kedu Plain. Return to Yogyakarta city and stop at Beringharjo Market — a vast three-floor covered traditional market where batik fabric, Javanese spices, traditional jamu medicines and street food stalls create an exhilarating sensory introduction to Java. Walk the legendary Malioboro Street for your first evening of batik shirts, wayang puppets and silver keepsakes before dinner at a Malioboro warung and hotel check-in.
suggestedActivities: Borobudur early afternoon for manageable crowds
Climb all nine levels of Borobudur to the uppermost circular terraces
Beringharjo Market for Javanese spices, batik and street food
Evening batik shopping on Malioboro Street

days.title: Mount Merapi Jeep Lava Tour — Prambanan Hindu Temple — Kraton Palace — Taman Sari Water Castle
days.description: The most culturally and volcanically intense day of the trip. After breakfast, board an open 4WD jeep for the Mount Merapi Lava Tour — bouncing up hardened lava fields to the haunting Kaliadem bunker (buried by 2010's pyroclastic flow), the otherworldly Batu Alien rock formation, and the Mini Museum of Merapi filled with eruption artefacts and survivor testimonies. After the jeep adventure, lunch at a local restaurant before heading east to Prambanan. The UNESCO-listed Prambanan compound's three central towers dedicated to Shiva, Vishnu and Brahma soar to 47 metres above the Javanese plain, their facades covered in extraordinary Ramayana relief carvings — your guide will walk you through the narrative sequence. If a Ramayana Ballet evening performance is scheduled (Tue/Thu/Sat), your guide can advise on ticket arrangements as a personal add-on. Continue to the Kraton Yogyakarta for a tour of the living royal palace, then conclude the cultural immersion at Taman Sari Water Castle — the enchanting 18th-century royal pleasure garden with its ornate bathing pools, underground mosque, and batik-artists' alleyways. Dinner and overnight.
suggestedActivities: Morning Merapi Jeep Tour before the heat builds
Prambanan guide for detailed Ramayana relief panel narration
Ramayana Ballet at Prambanan open-air theatre (own expense, seasonal)
Taman Sari underground mosque — a hidden architectural gem
Browse batik artists at work in the Taman Sari community

days.title: Timang Beach — Hobbit House — Pengger Pine Forest
days.description: Head southeast into the Gunungkidul karst highlands for a day of coastal drama and forest magic. First stop is Timang Beach, where sheer white limestone cliffs drop straight into the crashing Indian Ocean — one of the most dramatic coastal vistas in all of Java. The brave can attempt the optional hand-pulled wooden gondola crossing (RM65 per person) to tiny Watu Panjang Island or cross via the suspension bridge (RM33 per person). After a cliff-side lunch, visit the famous Hobbit House — a Lord of the Rings-inspired grass-roofed structure nestled in the Gunungkidul hills that has become a favourite for families and creative travellers. As the afternoon light softens, wind through the atmospheric Pinus Pengger pine forest on the Dlingo ridge, where tall pine trees frame stunning silhouettes over the Yogyakarta plain and the distant volcanoes. Return to the hotel for dinner and rest.
suggestedActivities: Timang Beach gondola crossing (optional, RM65/person)
Timang Beach suspension bridge (optional, RM33/person)
Hobbit House photos and exploration
Sunset silhouette photography at Pinus Pengger pine grove

days.title: Dieng Plateau Full-Day Expedition — Ancient Temples, Sikidang Crater & Telaga Warna
days.description: Today's journey is a full-day expedition to the Dieng Plateau — the 'Land Above the Clouds' — in Wonosobo regency, approximately 3 hours from Yogyakarta. The drive itself is spectacular, ascending through terraced vegetable farms, misty mountain passes, and highland villages before arriving at the plateau at 2,000 metres above sea level. The plateau's flat highland fields are scattered with the oldest surviving Hindu temples in Java — the Arjuna Temple Complex (a cluster of five temples dating from the 7th century CE) predates both Borobudur and Prambanan by over a century. Explore also the Dwarawati and Gatotkaca temples before walking to the Sikidang Crater, an active volcanic caldera whose sulphurous fumaroles and steaming mud pools bubble at the surface of the crater floor — you can walk remarkably close to the active vents. The highlight for many visitors is Telaga Warna (Colour Lake), whose waters shift between turquoise, green, yellow and golden depending on the sulphur mineral concentration and the angle of light — the lakeside boardwalk at midday often reveals the most vivid colours. Return to Yogyakarta for a well-earned dinner and overnight.
suggestedActivities: Early morning departure to reach Dieng plateau before cloud cover builds
Arjuna Temple Complex — Java's oldest surviving Hindu temples
Sikidang Crater active volcanic fumaroles walkway
Telaga Warna Colour Lake photography from the boardwalk
Try Dieng's famous Mie Ongklok noodle soup and carica fruit jam

days.title: Free Exploration — Departure
days.description: Enjoy a leisurely final breakfast at the hotel before making the most of your remaining hours in Yogyakarta. Options include a return visit to Malioboro Street for any last-minute batik or silver shopping, a batik-making class at one of the Prawirotaman studios, a relaxed breakfast at one of the city's renowned coffee shops, or a visit to the nearby Affandi Museum — the private collection of Indonesia's most celebrated expressionist painter, housed in his extraordinary riverside studio home. At the appointed time, your private driver collects you for the transfer to Yogyakarta International Airport (YIA) for your departure flight home to Malaysia.
suggestedActivities: Final Malioboro Street and Beringharjo Market shopping
Batik-making class at a Prawirotaman studio (own expense)
Visit the Affandi Museum — Indonesia's greatest expressionist painter
Java coffee tasting at a Jogja specialty café

whyBook.heading: Why Book Your 5-Day Yogyakarta & Dieng Plateau Trip Through Dhesu
whyBook.body: The Dieng Plateau day trip requires careful timing and logistical coordination — an early departure and the right local contacts make the difference between a magnificent day and a frustrating one. Dhesu Travel's experienced Yogyakarta team has been running this extended itinerary for over 30 years and ensures every moment of your 5-day journey is perfectly paced.

sections.heading: 5 Days Yogyakarta Tour Package from Malaysia — The Complete Java Experience
sections.body: For Malaysian travellers with 5 days to spare, this itinerary represents the most comprehensive possible introduction to Central Java. Yogyakarta is endlessly layered — most visitors scratch the surface with Borobudur and Malioboro and leave feeling they've only seen a fraction of what's available. This package ensures you experience the full depth: two UNESCO World Heritage temple complexes, an active volcano, a dramatic coastline, a haunted pine forest, and the ancient mystical plateau of Dieng — arguably the most atmospheric destination in the entire Java travel circuit. The Dieng Plateau has been drawing Javanese Hindu pilgrims since at least the 7th century CE and continues to produce a sense of otherworldly reverence in modern travellers. At 2,000 metres above sea level, surrounded by volcanic craters and colour-shifting lakes, it is entirely unlike anywhere else in Southeast Asia. Flying from Kuala Lumpur (or Penang, or Johor Bahru), the journey to Yogyakarta takes just 2 hours — making this 5-day package an outstanding value long-weekend extension for Malaysians.

sections.heading: The Dieng Plateau — Java's Ancient Highland Mystery
sections.body: The Dieng Plateau is a volcanic highland caldera in the Wonosobo regency of Central Java, sitting at an altitude of 1,900 to 2,100 metres above sea level. Its name derives from the Javanese 'di hyang' meaning 'abode of the gods' or 'place of the divine', and the ancient Hindus who first settled the plateau in the 6th century CE clearly regarded it as sacred ground. The Arjuna Temple Complex — a cluster of five small 7th-century Shaivite Hindu shrines — is the oldest surviving Hindu temple group in Java, predating the great monuments of Borobudur and Prambanan by over a century. These temples were built during the early Mataram kingdom and were likely used as royal meditation and ceremonial sites given the plateau's remote altitude and volcanic energy. The Dieng area remains volcanically very active: the Sikidang Crater allows visitors to walk right up to active sulphurous fumaroles and boiling mud pools. Telaga Warna, the famous Colour Lake, owes its remarkable hue-shifting properties to dissolved sulphur compounds that interact with light differently throughout the day. Early morning visits (the plateau is often above the cloud layer by 9am) reveal the lake in its most vivid green-turquoise state, while late morning sunlight produces golden reflections across the mineral-rich surface.

items.question: Do I need a visa to visit Indonesia from Malaysia?
items.answer: Malaysian passport holders enjoy visa-free entry to Indonesia for stays of up to 30 days. No advance visa application is required.

items.question: How far is the Dieng Plateau from Yogyakarta?
items.answer: The Dieng Plateau is approximately 120 km northwest of Yogyakarta in the Wonosobo regency, about a 3-hour drive. We depart early in the morning to maximise time on the plateau and avoid afternoon clouds. The drive through the highland terraces and mountain passes is itself a scenic highlight.

items.question: Is the Dieng Plateau cold?
items.answer: Yes — at 2,000 metres above sea level, Dieng's temperatures typically range between 10°C and 22°C during the day, dropping significantly in the evenings. We recommend bringing a light jacket or windbreaker for the plateau visit. The lower-altitude Yogyakarta city itself is warm and tropical.

items.question: What is the best time of day to visit the Dieng Plateau?
items.answer: Morning is ideal. The plateau is often above the cloud layer by 9am on clear days, giving the best visibility across the ancient temples and Telaga Warna. By late afternoon, cloud cover frequently rolls in. We depart Yogyakarta early on Day 4 to make the most of the morning conditions.

items.question: Can I upgrade to 4-star hotels?
items.answer: Yes. The 4-star supplement adds RM100 per person (4-star total: RM1,950). Properties include Abadi Hotel Yogyakarta and Grand Zuri Malioboro — both centrally located near Malioboro Street with excellent facilities.

items.question: Are all meals included?
items.answer: Yes — all meals from Day 1 lunch through to Day 5 breakfast are included in the package price. This includes breakfast, lunch and dinner daily except Day 1 (lunch and dinner only, as you fly in) and Day 5 (breakfast only, as you fly out).

items.question: Is this package suitable for first-time visitors to Indonesia?
items.answer: Absolutely — in fact, it's our top recommendation for first-timers who have 5 days. It covers all the iconic highlights while adding the Dieng Plateau dimension that most tours miss entirely. Your English-speaking private guide handles all logistics, so no local language knowledge is required.
